title: kyutai-labs/pocket-tts
friendlyTitle: A small text-to-speech tool that can run on a CPU
summary: Kyutai Labs’ pocket-tts is a Python project for text-to-speech designed to fit on a CPU. The repository describes it as a TTS that fits in your pocket, and GitHub currently shows about 6k stars and 633 forks.
friendlySummary: pocket-tts is an open-source project for turning text into speech without needing a large cloud setup.
whyItMatters: Developers can use it to explore lightweight speech generation for local assistants, accessibility tools, offline voice interfaces, and privacy-sensitive applications that should not depend on cloud TTS calls.
friendlyWhyItMatters: Use it to experiment with local voice features, such as reading text aloud or adding speech to an offline assistant.
